title: Chen_2021
study_design: Retrospective cohort study using a publicly accessible critical care database (MIMIC-III)

sample_size.source_quote: Finally, the total number of 27 131 patients were included (figure 1).

patient_age_mean.source_quote: The mean age of the study population was 64.00±17.94 years.

icu_mortality_percent.source_quote: Overall, 3776 patients (13.92%) of the study population died within 28 days after ICU admission.

mortality_predictors.source_quote: Compared with patients of G2, patients of G1, G5 and G6 had increased 28-day ICU mortality with a non-adjusted HR (G1: HR=1.15, 95% CI 1.00 to 1.32, p=0.0481; G5: HR=1.34, 95% CI 1.18 to 1.52, p<0.0001; G6: HR=2.50, 95% CI 2.25 to 2.79, p<0.0001, respectively).

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Serum phosphate group G5 (very-high-normal, ≥4.0 mg/dL and <4.5 mg/dL)
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Compared with patients of G2, patients of G1, G5 and G6 had increased 28-day ICU mortality with a non-adjusted HR (G1: HR=1.15, 95% CI 1.00 to 1.32, p=0.0481; G5: HR=1.34, 95% CI 1.18 to 1.52, p<0.0001; G6: HR=2.50, 95% CI 2.25 to 2.79, p<0.0001, respectively).

Compared with patients of G2, patients of G1, G5 and G6 had increased 28-day ICU mortality with a non-adjusted HR (G1: HR=1.15, 95% CI 1.00 to 1.32, p=0.0481; G5: HR=1.34, 95% CI 1.18 to 1.52, p<0.0001; G6: HR=2.50, 95% CI 2.25 to 2.79, p<0.0001, respectively).

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Serum phosphate group G5 (very-high-normal, ≥4.0 mg/dL and <4.5 mg/dL)
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, gender, ethnicities, heart rate, mean arterial pressure, respiratory rate, temperature, oxygen saturation
mortality_predictors.source_quote: After being adjusted by the model 1, compared with patients of G2, patients of G5 and G6 had increased 28-day ICU mortality with a HR ((G5: HR=1.32, 95% CI 1.16 to 1.50, p<0.0001; G6: HR=2.20, 95% CI 1.97 to 2.45, p<0.0001, respectively), adjusted by model 1).

mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, gender, ethnicities, heart rate, mean arterial pressure, respiratory rate, temperature, oxygen saturation
mortality_predictors.source_quote: After being adjusted by the model 1, compared with patients of G2, patients of G5 and G6 had increased 28-day ICU mortality with a HR ((G5: HR=1.32, 95% CI 1.16 to 1.50, p<0.0001; G6: HR=2.20, 95% CI 1.97 to 2.45, p<0.0001, respectively), adjusted by model 1).

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Serum phosphate group G4 (high-normal, ≥3.5 mg/dL and <4.0 mg/dL)
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, gender, ethnicities, heart rate, mean arterial pressure, respiratory rate, temperature, oxygen saturation, admission types, ICU types, SOFA score, SAPS II, OASIS score, congestive heart failure, coronary artery disease, hypertension, diabetes, chronic pulmonary disease, atrial fibrillation, liver disease, fluid and electrolyte disorder, malignant tumour, chronic kidney disease, serum sodium, serum potassium, serum calcium, serum creatinine, blood urea nitrogen, serum bicarbonate, serum chloride, white cell count, haemoglobin, platelet, use of mechanical ventilation, use of renal replacement therapy, use of vasopressor
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Besides, we found that the G4 had a beneficial effect on the 28-day ICU mortality (HR=0.87, 95% CI 0.77 to 0.98, p=0.0243, adjusted by model 2).

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Serum phosphate group G5 (very-high-normal, ≥4.0 mg/dL and <4.5 mg/dL)
mortality_predictors.source_quote: The relationship between serum phosphate and 90-day ICU mortality had a similar pattern to that of 28-day ICU mortality, with increased risk for events in patients of G5 and G6 with a non-adjusted HR (G5: HR=1.31, 95% CI 1.18 to 1.46, p<0.0001; G6: HR=2.32, 95% CI 2.12 to 2.54, p<0.0001, respectively).

The relationship between serum phosphate and 90-day ICU mortality had a similar pattern to that of 28-day ICU mortality, with increased risk for events in patients of G5 and G6 with a non-adjusted HR (G5: HR=1.31, 95% CI 1.18 to 1.46, p<0.0001; G6: HR=2.32, 95% CI 2.12 to 2.54, p<0.0001, respectively).

mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, gender, ethnicities, heart rate, mean arterial pressure, respiratory rate, temperature, oxygen saturation
mortality_predictors.source_quote: After being adjusted by the model 1, compared with patients of G2, patients of G1, G5 and G6 had increased 90-day ICU mortality with a HR ((G1: HR=1.13, 95% CI 1.01 to 1.27, p=0.0372; G5: HR=1.30, 95% CI 1.17 to 1.44, p<0.0001; G6: HR=2.09, 95% CI 1.91 to 2.30, p<0.0001, respectively), adjusted by model 1).

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Serum phosphate group G6 (hyperphosphataemia, ≥4.5 mg/dL) in MICU patients
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, gender, ethnicities, heart rate, mean arterial pressure, respiratory rate, temperature, oxygen saturation, admission types, ICU types, SOFA score, SAPS II, OASIS score, comorbidities, laboratory results, interventions
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Nevertheless, at the MICU, patients of G6 had increased risk of 28 or 90-day mortality ((HR=1.37, 95% CI 1.15 to 1.63, p=0.0005; HR=1.25, 95% CI 1.08 to 1.44, p=0.0035, respectively), adjusted by model 2).

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Serum phosphate group G6 (hyperphosphataemia, ≥4.5 mg/dL) in MICU patients
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, gender, ethnicities, heart rate, mean arterial pressure, respiratory rate, temperature, oxygen saturation, admission types, ICU types, SOFA score, SAPS II, OASIS score, comorbidities, laboratory results, interventions
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Nevertheless, at the MICU, patients of G6 had increased risk of 28 or 90-day mortality ((HR=1.37, 95% CI 1.15 to 1.63, p=0.0005; HR=1.25, 95% CI 1.08 to 1.44, p=0.0035, respectively), adjusted by model 2).

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Serum phosphate group G4 (high-normal, ≥3.5 mg/dL and <4.0 mg/dL) in SICU patients
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, gender, ethnicities, heart rate, mean arterial pressure, respiratory rate, temperature, oxygen saturation, admission types, ICU types, SOFA score, SAPS II, OASIS score, comorbidities, laboratory results, interventions
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Moreover, at the SICU, a significant inverse association between patients of G4 and 28 or 90-day mortality was observed ((HR=0.64, 95% CI 0.48 to 0.84, p=0.0017; HR=0.72, 95% CI 0.57 to 0.91, p=0.0067, respectively), adjusted by model 2).

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Serum phosphate group G6 (hyperphosphataemia, ≥4.5 mg/dL) in SICU patients
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, gender, ethnicities, heart rate, mean arterial pressure, respiratory rate, temperature, oxygen saturation, admission types, ICU types, SOFA score, SAPS II, OASIS score, comorbidities, laboratory results, interventions
mortality_predictors.source_quote: hyperphosphataemia (G6) exerted a beneficial effect on the 28 or 90-day mortality of patients at the SICU ((HR=0.54, 95% CI 0.40 to 0.74, p<0.0001; HR=0.69, 95% CI 0.53 to 0.89, p=0.0042, respectively), adjusted by model 2)

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Serum phosphate group G4 (high-normal, ≥3.5 mg/dL and <4.0 mg/dL) in female patients
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, gender, ethnicities, heart rate, mean arterial pressure, respiratory rate, temperature, oxygen saturation, admission types, ICU types, SOFA score, SAPS II, OASIS score, comorbidities, laboratory results, interventions
mortality_predictors.source_quote: we found that the G4 had a beneficial effect on the 28-day ICU mortality for female (HR=0.82, 95% CI 0.68 to 0.97, p=0.0244, adjusted by model 2).
